title: The English Works of Wyclif Hitherto Unprinted
summary: aeh6713 is the repository XML for a 2006 University of Michigan uncorrected OCR/Level-1 TEI encoding derived from Frederic David Matthew's 1880 edition of twenty-eight numbered Middle English Wycliffite tracts. The XML verbally encodes title/printer matter and tracts on pp. 1–482 while linking most preliminaries and pp. 483–572 only as page images. Matthew's edition draws base text from nine identified codices and collates five of them in additional tract-scoped roles; no manuscript is represented as containing the whole twenty-eight-tract compilation. Complete exposed IIIF inventories are cached for CCCC MS 296 and John Rylands English MS 86, the Bodley 788 cache is explicitly selected and target-absent, and TCD MS 244 is retained as metadata-only because bot mitigation prevented a reliable canvas audit. Rights are separated across medieval objects, 1880 edition, provider-described 1975 reprint, U-M encoding and page images, repository artifact, catalogs, and modern facsimiles.
review_notes: Matthew's umbrella Wyclif attribution is historical edition metadata, not a present authorship conclusion for all twenty-eight tracts.
Every manuscript-source relation is tract- or passage-scoped. Complete facsimile coverage is qualified relative to the provider's exposed inventory.
Matthew's printed Corpus shelfmark '290' is preserved as historical wording/error; the exact current codex and content match CCCC MS 296.
The explicit Sidney Sussex tract-XII collation statement governs the graph; the contradictory front-list 'No. XX' remains unresolved.
Physical manuscript ownership, textual public-domain status, editorial copyright, catalog metadata, page-image licensing, and access terms are not conflated.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for aeh6713
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: The internal U-M identifier is AEH6713.0001.001. The repository artifact preserves a 2006 legacy availability notice that is less specific than the current public U-M item rights statement.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item AEH6713.0001.001
entities.description: A 2006 uncorrected OCR text with Level-1 TEI markup and links to 638 page images. The verbal XML covers title/printer matter and the twenty-eight tracts on printed pp. 1–482; most preliminaries and all back matter on pp. 483–572 are linked by page milestones but not transcribed.
notes: The U-M discovery record's Wyclif author field is inherited attribution metadata, not a modern tract-by-tract authorship conclusion.

entities.label: University of Michigan page images linked from AEH6713.0001.001
entities.description: The 638 page images identified by PB milestones in the U-M encoding, covering the scanned book beyond the portions verbally OCR-transcribed.

entities.label: Frederic David Matthew, The English Works of Wyclif Hitherto Unprinted (1880)
entities.description: Matthew's Early English Text Society edition-level compilation of twenty-eight numbered Middle English tracts, editorial arguments and notes, an appendix, and a glossarial index.

bibliographic.citation: Frederic David Matthew, ed., The English Works of Wyclif Hitherto Unprinted, Early English Text Society, Original Series 74 (London: published for the Early English Text Society by Trübner & Co., 1880), li + [1] + 572 pp., frontispiece.
bibliographic.title: The English Works of Wyclif Hitherto Unprinted
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 74
bibliographic.publisher: Trübner & Co. for the Early English Text Society

physical_description.extent: li preliminary pages, one unnumbered leaf, 572 Arabic-numbered pages, and a frontispiece
notes: The frontispiece reproduces part of Lambeth Palace Library MS 551, leaf 16.

entities.label: Provider-described Kraus 1975 reprint of Matthew's 1880 edition
entities.description: A qualified reprint entity created because Internet Archive metadata describes the scanned carrier as 'Edition: Kraus 1975,' while visual inspection shows the original 1880 title page and pagination and no inspected reprint statement.

date_statements.label: provider-described reprint date
date_statements.notes: The exact issue and physical copy statement remain unverified.

bibliographic.citation: Kraus reprint, 1975, of Matthew's The English Works of Wyclif Hitherto Unprinted (1880); exact issue statement unverified.
bibliographic.title: The English Works of Wyclif Hitherto Unprinted

entities.label: Internet Archive scan englishworksofwy00wycl
entities.description: A 646-page complete access PDF and derivatives of the provider-described Kraus 1975 carrier, visually preserving Matthew's 1880 title page, frontispiece, preliminaries, tracts, appendix, notes, and glossarial index.
entities.reproduction_format: PDF page scan with OCR and scan-data derivatives
notes: Internet Archive identifies University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign as contributor and states: 'In public domain. Work published prior to 1925.'

entities.label: Corpus Christi College, Cambridge, MS 296
alternate_labels: Matthew's printed 'Corpus Christi College, Cambridge, MS 290'
entities.description: The current exact identity of Matthew's X and base witness for tracts I–XX. The relevant tract sequence occupies discontiguous manuscript page ranges; this codex does not contain the complete twenty-eight-tract edition.

historical_designations: printed as MS 290 in Matthew's front manuscript list

physical_description.extent: ff. 3 + 150; 306 exposed digital surfaces labeled f. ir through p. 298
notes: Current Parker cataloging describes a collection once attributed entirely to Wyclif but now believed to be by multiple authors.

entities.label: Parker Library complete exposed IIIF object for CCCC MS 296
entities.description: All 306 surfaces exposed by the current IIIF manifest, cached at 1600-pixel width. Complete describes the provider inventory, not every physical feature of the codex.

entities.label: Trinity College Dublin, MS 244
entities.description: A Wycliffite miscellaneous-tract codex used by Matthew as base for tract XXI and as a collated witness for tracts I–X, XIII–XVIII, and XXV.

notes: The current DataCite description reports, but also documents scholarly contestation of, an identification of the hand as Adam Pinkhurst.

entities.label: DataCite metadata record for Trinity College Dublin MS 244
entities.description: A 2024 item-level metadata record describing the digitized manuscript, contents, subjects, and contested scribal attribution. Its rightsList is empty and it is not an auditable image inventory.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Bodley 647
entities.description: The additional Oxford witness Matthew says he collated for tract III.

notes: Current cataloging places its production in the English West Midlands. No public online images are exposed.

entities.label: Sidney Sussex College, Cambridge, MS 74
entities.description: The fifteenth-century codex whose Ave Maria exposition begins at f. 189v and was explicitly collated by Matthew for tract XII. Matthew's front list instead says 'No. XX,' a discrepancy retained as unresolved.

holding.notes: The exact current identity and contents are established through James's catalog; the separate Sidney access page supplies the enquiry route.

notes: The Ave Maria exposition begins f. 189v; the following six-sermon unit begins f. 191v, placing the target approximately at ff. 189v–191r.

entities.label: M. R. James, catalogue of Sidney Sussex manuscripts (1895)
entities.description: The complete printed catalog containing entry 74 for A.4.12, now Sidney Sussex MS 74, with its physical description and contents.

bibliographic.citation: M. R. James, A Descriptive Catalogue of the Manuscripts in the Library of Sidney Sussex College, Cambridge (Cambridge: University Press, 1895), no. 74, pp. 52–53.
bibliographic.title: A Descriptive Catalogue of the Manuscripts in the Library of Sidney Sussex College, Cambridge

entities.label: Trinity College Dublin, MS 245
entities.description: Matthew's CC, base witness for tracts XXII and XXIII and collated witness for tract XXIV. It is not on TCD's current public list of digitized medieval manuscripts.

holding.notes: No exact current public item record or complete facsimile was found; the old-to-current pressmark is supported by scholarship.

entities.label: John Rylands Library, English MS 86
entities.description: A parchment codex used by Matthew as base for tracts XXVII and XXVIII and collated for tract XXIII. Those three tracts occupy separate folio runs and do not constitute the whole codex.

date_statements.label: current catalog date
date_statements.value: end of the 14th or beginning of the 15th century

physical_description.dimensions: 158 × 110 mm, with smaller and wider exceptions noted by the catalog
notes: One current anglicana hand throughout.

entities.label: University of Manchester catalog record for English MS 86
entities.description: The item-level JSON catalog record and logical-structure inventory that identify the manuscript and exact folio/canvas ranges for the three relevant Middle English tracts.

entities.label: University of Manchester complete exposed IIIF object for English MS 86
entities.description: All 262 canvases exposed by the current IIIF manifest, including covers, flyleaves, and manuscript leaves, cached at 1600-pixel width. The corpus tracts occupy three discontiguous runs.

entities.label: New College, Oxford, MS 95
entities.description: Matthew's Q and base witness for tract XXIV, Of Faith, Hope and Charity.

holding.notes: A New College collection feature identifies MS 95; no complete public facsimile was found, and rare-books and manuscripts access is requestable.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Bodley 788
entities.description: Matthew's A and base witness for tract XXV, De Sacramento Altaris. Digital Bodleian exposes only ten selected images of ff. 1r–5v, which do not contain the target tract.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian selected images of MS Bodley 788
entities.description: The complete current provider inventory of ten selected images, ff. 1r–5v. This is partial coverage of the manuscript and has verified zero overlap with tract XXV.

entities.label: Lambeth Palace Library, MS 551
entities.description: The sole manuscript Matthew names for tract XXVI. He describes a 59-leaf parchment codex containing the tract; a frontispiece reproduces part of leaf 16.

date_statements.label: date reported by Matthew
date_statements.notes: Matthew reports the opinion of a contemporary expert; a current item-level catalog record was not publicly available.

holding.notes: Commercial microfilm reel 14 lists MS 551; no exact current public catalog record or complete public digital facsimile was found.

physical_description.format: Codex containing the tract printed as Matthew no. XXVI

entities.label: Matthew frontispiece of Lambeth MS 551, leaf 16
entities.description: A single monochrome facsimile plate reproducing part of MS 551, leaf 16, bound as the 1880 edition's frontispiece. It is not a complete manuscript surrogate.

entities.label: World Microfilms Lambeth medieval-manuscripts reel 14
entities.description: A commercial microfilm reel whose product list includes Lambeth MS 551. The exact image count, physical coverage, and target completeness were not audited.

identifiers.value: Lambeth Palace Library: The Medieval Manuscripts, reel 14

rights.component: Repository XML artifact and inherited data layer
rights.basis: The pinned CME root LICENSE uses Unlicense wording for 'software,' but it does not expressly establish applicability to inherited U-M XML, text, or data. The artifact also embeds a legacy U-M copyright-may-apply notice.
notes: Do not silently extend a software license to inherited textual or data layers.

rights.component: Current U-M CME item text and presentation
rights.basis: The current item page labels the resource Public Domain and links No Copyright – United States.

rights.component: Legacy 2006 availability statement embedded in the XML header
rights.basis: The source XML says, 'Where applicable, subject to copyright' and warns that other distribution restrictions may apply.
notes: This records a dated notice, not a current legal conclusion; the current item-specific NoC-US statement is more specific.

rights.component: U-M-linked page images as offered with the CME item
rights.basis: The current item page applies the Public Domain / No Copyright–United States statement to the offered item.

rights.component: Matthew's 1880 editorial text, arrangement, notes, and medieval texts reproduced within it
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1880; U-M and Internet Archive both provide item-specific public-domain assertions.

rights.component: Any new 1975 reprint typesetting, paratext, or edition-specific contribution
rights.basis: The exact 1975 issue statement and any new reprint contribution have not been inspected; public-domain status of the 1880 source does not decide rights in possible later additions.

rights.component: Internet Archive PDF, OCR, scan data, and item delivery
rights.basis: Internet Archive item metadata expressly states 'In public domain. Work published prior to 1925.'

rights.component: Parker Library MS 296 images and metadata
rights.basis: The current Parker manifest and catalog license the digital object under CC BY-NC 4.0.

rights.component: Item-level DataCite metadata record
rights.basis: The exact DataCite response has an empty rightsList. TCD's general digitized-content policy cannot be substituted for missing item-level rights metadata.

rights.component: TCD Digital Collections manuscript photography and viewer delivery
rights.basis: TCD's general policy conditionally applies CC BY 4.0 to digitized out-of-copyright items but identifies exceptions; the exact MS 244 item rightsList is empty and the viewer could not be audited.
notes: Do not treat the general policy as an exact item-level license.

rights.component: James's 1895 catalog text
rights.basis: The catalog was published in 1895.

rights.component: Internet Archive PDF and OCR delivery
rights.basis: The underlying 1895 catalog is public domain and the derivatives are publicly downloadable; no separate item-level reusable-file license was found.

rights.component: Downloadable University of Manchester metadata
rights.basis: The item-level record states that downloadable images and metadata are licensed CC BY-NC 4.0.

rights.component: Images and metadata made available for download
rights.basis: The item-level record licenses downloaded images and metadata under CC BY-NC 4.0.

rights.component: Zooming viewer images
rights.basis: The item-level record separately states 'Zooming image © University of Manchester Library, All rights reserved.'
notes: The provider distinguishes zooming-viewer display from images made available for download.

rights.component: Digital Bodleian selected images and metadata
rights.basis: The Digital Bodleian manifest attributes the object under CC BY-NC 4.0.

rights.component: Printed 1880 frontispiece and its Internet Archive scan
rights.basis: The plate was published with the 1880 edition, and Internet Archive expressly marks the scanned item public domain.

rights.component: Commercial microfilm images and product delivery
rights.basis: The public product page identifies the reel but does not expose a reusable-image license for the MS 551 segment.

rights.component: Physical medieval manuscript as such
rights.basis: Copyright and license assessments attach to textual, editorial, catalog, and reproduction layers, not to ownership of the physical medieval object as such.

editorial_practices.description: Matthew assembled twenty-eight numbered Middle English tracts, supplied editorial arguments, and appended notes and a glossarial index.
Matthew states base and comparison manuscripts tract by tract; the lineage relations distinguish transcription from comparison and preserve missing or displaced passages.
editorial_practices.scope: Tracts I–XXVIII as individually scoped in the relations
notes: A collation relation never by itself makes the comparison witness the printed base.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Complete printed volume with front matter, twenty-eight tracts, appendix, notes, and glossarial index
editorial_practices.representation: Verbal OCR for title/printer matter and pp. 1–482; PB/EPB image links without verbal transcription for most front matter and pp. 483–572
editorial_practices.description: The U-M XML selectively OCR-transcribes the central tract sequence while retaining page-image linkage across the larger book.

evidence.citation: General Corpus source XML for aeh6713
evidence.summary: Establishes repository identity, U-M identifier, bibliographic source, 2006 OCR/TEI statement, legacy availability notice, PB/EPB linkage, and encoded text.

evidence.citation: Pinned Kyvero-Press/CME root LICENSE
evidence.summary: Contains Unlicense wording directed to 'software'; it does not expressly resolve applicability to inherited U-M XML, text, or data.

evidence.citation: Structural and textual inspection of aeh6713 XML
evidence.locator: Manual review plus XPath checks of TITLEPAGE, FRONT and BACK PB/EPB milestones, BODY/DIV1 1–28, and element counts
evidence.summary: Manual and XPath/count verification establishes title/printer transcription, the 28-tract verbal boundary through printed p. 482, untranscribed image-linked preliminaries and back matter, source statements, and exact representation metrics.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, The English Works of Wyclif Hitherto Unprinted
evidence.summary: Confirms stable item identity, source-edition citation, inherited author field, 2006 OCR production, public access, and current Public Domain / No Copyright–United States rights statement.

evidence.citation: Frederic David Matthew, ed., The English Works of Wyclif Hitherto Unprinted (1880), complete Internet Archive scan
evidence.locator: frontispiece; title page; manuscript list; contents; tract arguments; pp. 1–572
evidence.summary: Direct evidence for the edition boundary, tract titles and page ranges, base and collated witnesses, editorial statements, and front/back matter.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for englishworksofwy00wycl
evidence.summary: Preserves the item identifier, University of Illinois contributor, exact public-domain statement, and provider description 'Edition: Kraus 1975.'

evidence.citation: Visual inspection of the complete englishworksofwy00wycl PDF
evidence.locator: PDF pp. 8–11 and scan-wide page mapping
evidence.summary: Confirms the Lambeth MS 551 leaf-16 frontispiece, original 1880 title page, printer verso, contents, 646-page scan extent, and absence of an inspected reprint statement near the front.

evidence.citation: Parker Library on the Web, Corpus Christi College Cambridge MS 296
evidence.locator: catalog description and IIIF canvases 1–306; target manuscript pp. 1–39, 65–144, 160–221, 234–239, 297–298
evidence.summary: Confirms current shelfmark, post-1383 late-fourteenth-century date, multi-author Wycliffite context, exact tract page runs, complete exposed IIIF inventory, and CC BY-NC 4.0 license.

evidence.citation: DataCite record for Trinity College Dublin MS 244
evidence.summary: Confirms current shelfmark, digitized-object identity, detailed contents, 2024 record publication, contested Adam Pinkhurst attribution, and an empty rightsList; it supplies no canvas inventory.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Dublin, Digitised Manuscripts
evidence.summary: Confirms MS 244 on the current public digitized-manuscript list and its fifteenth-century Wycliffite description; MS 245 is absent from the list.

evidence.citation: The Library of Trinity College Dublin, Rights Information
evidence.summary: Explains TCD's conditional general licensing policy and exceptions; it is not substituted for MS 244's missing item-level rightsList.

evidence.citation: Estelle V. Stubbs, 'Seeking Scribal Communities in Medieval London,' in Scribal Cultures in Late Medieval England (2022)
evidence.summary: Supports the old-to-current TCD pressmark crosswalk C.III.12 = MS 244 and C.V.6 = MS 245; Matthew remains the direct evidence for tract-level editorial use.

evidence.citation: Manuscripts and Archives at Oxford University records for MS Bodley 647 and MS Bodley 788
evidence.summary: Confirms exact shelfmarks, dates, supports, Bodley 647's West Midlands context and lack of online images, and Bodley 788's ten selected-image extent.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian IIIF manifest for MS Bodley 788 selected images
evidence.summary: Confirms the complete current selected-image inventory, zero overlap with tract XXV, image identifiers, and CC BY-NC 4.0 attribution.

evidence.citation: M. R. James, A Descriptive Catalogue of the Manuscripts in the Library of Sidney Sussex College, Cambridge (1895)
evidence.summary: Confirms current numeric MS 74, historical A.4.12, fifteenth-century vellum codex of 207 folios, and the Ave Maria at f. 189v followed by six sermons at f. 191v.

evidence.citation: Sidney Sussex College Library, Access
evidence.summary: Provides the advance-arrangement route and library contact for external manuscript researchers.

evidence.citation: New College, Oxford, Reformation manuscript feature and Rare Books and Manuscripts access
evidence.summary: Confirms New College MS 95 in the collection context and provides the request route; no complete public facsimile was found.

evidence.citation: World Microfilms, Lambeth Palace Library: The Medieval Manuscripts
evidence.summary: Confirms commercial microfilm availability for MS 551 on reel 14 but exposes no audited image inventory or item-level reusable-image license.

evidence.citation: University of Manchester Library, John Rylands English MS 86
evidence.locator: descriptive metadata; logical structures; 262-canvas IIIF inventory
evidence.summary: Confirms shelfmark, former Ashburnham designation, date, support, extent, tract folio/canvas loci, 262 exposed canvases, download CC BY-NC 4.0 statement, separate all-rights-reserved viewer statement, and reproduction route.

evidence.citation: Anne Hudson, 'A Wycliffite Scholar of the Early Fifteenth Century,' Studies in Church History Subsidia 4 (1985)
evidence.summary: Associates most of tract XXVI with an anonymous radical Wycliffite scholar and establishes that it predates that scholar's separate 1413–1414 tract.

evidence.citation: The Huntington Library, HM 503
evidence.summary: Distinguishes the tract recension printed from Lambeth MS 551 on Matthew pp. 359–404 from an extended sermon recension in HM 503 and three other manuscripts.

open_questions.question: Is the Internet Archive source copy demonstrably a specific Kraus 1975 reprint, and what new reprint matter, if any, does it contain?
next_steps: Inspect copy-specific cataloging or colophon evidence beyond the preserved 1880 title matter; retain the reprint and facsimile relations as qualified meanwhile.

open_questions.question: Why does Matthew's front manuscript list assign Sidney Sussex A.4.12 to no. XX while the tract XII argument explicitly assigns its Ave Maria collation there and tract XX says it comes only from CCCC MS 296?
next_steps: Inspect MS 74 and modern tract handlists; do not create a tract-XX Sidney relation without new evidence.
